characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Actually Pretty Funny: Even some of Homey's angriest rants will make him laugh. Case in point, one time had him comparing himself toKunta Kinteand he could barely finish it without stifling a chuckle. "Angry Black Man" Stereotype: A rather justified example: hisunspecifiedcrime was implied to be a frame up and sent him to prison for some time and led to him becoming a reluctant circus clown. Aside Glance: Pretty rare for himnotto do this. Breakout Character: He, along with Fire Marshall Bill and Blaine and Antoine, are the most recognizable characters and the only one to receive his own video game. Catchphrase: "I don't think so! Homey don't play that!"

characters.name: Homey D. Clown
characters.description: Actually Pretty Funny: Even some of Homey's angriest rants will make him laugh. Case in point, one time had him comparing himself toKunta Kinteand he could barely finish it without stifling a chuckle. "Angry Black Man" Stereotype: A rather justified example: hisunspecifiedcrime was implied to be a frame up and sent him to prison for some time and led to him becoming a reluctant circus clown. Aside Glance: Pretty rare for himnotto do this. Breakout Character: He, along with Fire Marshall Bill and Blaine and Antoine, are the most recognizable characters and the only one to receive his own video game. Catchphrase: "I don't think so! Homey don't play that!"

characters.name: Fire Marshal Bill Burns
characters.description: Breakout Character: He, Homey, and Blaine and Antoine are among the most popular and recognizable characters on the show. He evenmakes a surprise cameo inThe StingerofLiar Liar. Catchphrase: "LEMME SHOW YA SOMETHIN'!" and "Don't worry, folks! I am a fire marshal!" Early-Installment Weirdness: In his early sketches, he still had hair (all the explosions eventually burned what was left of it off), and his demonstrations were somehowlessdestructive (i.e., he was left in one piece after the inevitable explosion). Flanderization: He’s outrageously cartoonish to begin with, but in his first appearance he’s actually attempting to teach about fire safety and onlyaccidentallydestroys the school. Later skits cast him as deliberately destructive, often in ways that have nothing to do with fire at all. Friend to All Children: He thinks he's this, being very eager to showcase fire safety to schoolchildren and providing them with marshmallows after heburns down their school.

characters.name: Blaine Edwards & Antoine Merriweather
characters.description: Amusing Injuries: Blaine gets hit with a falling bean bag that knocks him out and somehow turns himstraight. Asucker punchfrom Antoine later though turns him back. Catchphrase: They always say the phrase "Hated it!" to express disdain for a particular book, film, show or piece of art, especially if it has to do with women. The Dandy: Played with; they're both obviously gay, but both of them wear fancy outfits alongsude several accessories at times, including handkerchiefs, decorated headbands and capes. Effeminate Misogynistic Guy: In spite of being gay, they both despise women and usually call them a variety of colorful names like "fish" and "scallop". They also dismiss any work that heavily features women with a "Hated it!" Expy: The duo's show format and their sense of bickering is modeled after that of Siskel & Ebert.

characters.name: Benita Buttrell
characters.description: Breaking the Fourth Wall: If not an outrightFourth-Wall Observer. The majority of her skits have her treating the viewer as a neighborhood acquaintance, though nobody else seems to notice them. Only a handful of skits defied this, due to having actual characters to play off, and even then, shestillbroke the fourth wall in one of them. The Friend Nobody Likes: Implied: whenever she's out in public, people either treat her with indifference or disdain. Gossipy Hens: Downplayed, as it's just her doing the gossiping, but she's just as effective. Hypocritical Humor: She ain't one to gossip, but...Just don't say nothin' bad about Ms. Jenkins near her (though eventhatdoesn't stop her from gossiping about her either, usually with the most vicious gossip).She claims to be above her neighbors and acquaintances, even claiming that she works to better herself, but is just as dim and untrustworthy as them. A running gag is her having nicer things (diamond necklace, long hair, etc.) that ends up being destroyed due to her wearing them while cooking and burning them up. She also sports tacky clothing and always has rollers in her hair while in public. Just don't say nothin' bad about Ms. Jenkins near her (though eventhatdoesn't stop her from gossiping about her either, usually with the most vicious gossip).

characters.name: Tom & Tom
characters.description: Comically Missing the Point: Neither of them realize how racist they come off or even when racism is being displayed towards them. Case in point, a country club owner thought that they would be "Jesse fans" (meaning Jesse Jackson), but they assumed he meant JesseHelmsand thought that Jackson was"the brother who kept the nose". Everyone Has Standards: Even they express disgust at Arizona refusing to recognizeDr. King's birthdayas a national holiday. Fanboy: Both of them are huge fans of journalist Bryant Gumbel and comedian Byron Allen. Jerkass: Even with them beingIgnorant of Their Own Ignorancewith how they treat fellow Black people, they also are unfriendly and condescending otherwise and throw out insults like calling their teammate on a game show a dummy since she lost them a round. The Whitest Black Guy: Both of them: they love and support Jesse Helms and Ronald Reagan while hating Jesse Jackson, affirmative action and other related issues. When a comic at a country club told a racist joke that they already knew the punchline to, they confirmed it because they wrote it themselves.

characters.name: Calhoun Tubbs
characters.description: Brutal Honesty: He always sings a song with no filter on or in a way that tells the truth, particularly one the the subject matter doesn't want to hear. Catchphrase:"Thank you very much!""Wrote a song about it. Like to hear it? Here it goes..." "Thank you very much!" "Wrote a song about it. Like to hear it? Here it goes..." Comically Missing the Point: He doesn't seem to realize how damaging and hurtful his lyrics are (he is an old man). During his first skit where he sung at the funeral of a "friend" of his, by the time he was finished singing, the angry relatives of the man tried to charge at him and he believed that they were giving him a standing ovation.

characters.name: Vera de Milo
characters.description: Baby Be Mine: In one sketch,The Fist that Rocks the Cradle, she works as a babysitter to a married couple with two children and begins to treat their baby as her own, even breastfeeding her.Unlike in the film, the mother notices the change in the baby when she gains muscles and has grown stubble to look like a younger version of Vera. Brawn Hilda: She's about as sexy as you'd expect Jim Carrey in a bikini to be. Gag Lips: One episode has her end up with theseafter burning her lips on a curling iron.The next scene has her sporting a pair of burned and blistered "full" lips. Girlish Pigtails: Vera is always seen wearing incredibly small ones. Kavorka Woman: She's exceptionally unattractive, but has no shortage of suitors who either pursue her or she pursues herself.

characters.name: Anton Jackson
characters.description: Attention Whore: Subverted;he's not a jerk about it,but he does command people's attention whenever he's around others. Cardboard Box Home: Obviously. He even has his ownhome-improvement showcalledThis Ol' Box. Crazy Homeless Person: Averted. In spite of his various eccentricities, he's actually quite insightful and very resourceful. Hidden Depths: Anton is quite talented overall; aside from his resourcefulness, he can do his taxes, can sing and act, is smart enough to negotiate more money from people and can defend himself physically. Prince For A Day: One sketch has him end up temporarily wealthy due to some escaping bank robbers throwing their money into a dumpster where he just so happened to be sleeping at the time.

characters.name: The Head Detective
characters.description: The Casanova: Women find him irresistible, with his first sketch ending in him engaging in a three-way. Catchphrase: "Justdoit!", usually when his partner is reluctant to get physical with him in order to get the perp. Dating Catwoman: One sketch has him dating a female robber, anactualMrs. Potato Head.After she kills herselfby jumping into a fryer,he is clearly distraught even as he and his partner eat her remains like french fries. Handicapped Badass: Losing his body is of no inconvenience to him, still being the lead detective and very effective at his job. Who Needs Their Whole Body?: Indeed. He just needs his hands and feet attached to his head and his partner to throw him. His last sketch consists of him being only a head.

characters.name: Ice Man & Whiz
characters.description: Blackmail: One scam has them willing to sell compromising pictures of celebrities. Case in point, they include a cardboard cutout of Bo Jackson in a dress and pearls they put on the figure and Whiz pretending to kiss him.Ice Man:I guess Bo Knows cross-dressing! Catchphrase: Whenever they talk about a sale or a good deal for their "customers" to get, they'll cry out "Mo' Money! Mo' Money! Mo' Money!" in unison. Con Men: Indeed they are, specifically of theGet-Rich-Quick SchemeandHonest John's Dealershipvarieties. Every Episode Ending: Every single one of their scams ends with them about to be caught (usually via an alarm blaring or an announcement of an intruder(s) on the premises), only for them to run off just in time. Impossible Theft: Among their greatest thefts include an ATM, an Academy Award, a Greyhound bus andMichael Jackson.

characters.name: Clavelle & Howard Tibbs III
characters.description: Heterosexual Life-Partners: They get on very well with each other and have the same goals of becoming famous through underhanded or low-quality means. High Hopes, Zero Talent: Both men wish to become famous directors and/or producers, but between their odd approach, the cheap quality of their "films" and dim intellect, they seldom succeed. Impossibly Tacky Clothes: Their fashion sense is both rather gaudy and outdated. Mad Libs Catch Phrase: Whenever Clavelle realizes he makes a mistake or runs into a discrepancy, he will often say, "Well, ain't that a X!" (blip, chocolate chip cookie, etc.) My Card: Howard will always pull out their business card to give to their client whilst giving a sudden and bombastic shout of "Bam!"

characters.name: Lil Magic
characters.description: Catchphrase: "I was Right On magazine's 'Miss Smile Bright 1987, see?!" (Smiles maniacally) and singing the opening line ofDreamgirls"And I Am Telling You (I'm Not Going)". High Hopes, Zero Talent: Lil Magic (and her Mom) have aspirations for more future, but she clearly cannot sing, dance or act. Motor Mouth: She always talks incessantly to the point of annoying her mother and being told to be quiet. Phrase Catcher: Via her mother: "Oh, HUSH UP!" and "Now that's my baby!" The Pollyanna: In spite of her lack of talent and perpetual rejection, she remains a happy and optimistic child.

characters.name: Oswald Bates
characters.description: Bait-and-Switch: The first clip has him speaking to the audience in a public service announcement lesson. Then the camera trucks back to show that he's inside a jail cell. Delusions of Eloquence: Definitely. Interestingly enough, none of the other prisoners (and First Lady Barbara Bush!) have a problem understanding him.Announcer:Understand what you read; he doesn't. In the Blood: He has a little boy who speaks just like him. Needless to say, he'sveryproud of him. Malaproper: His way of talking, such as mixing up "congress" and "condoms" and "taxi cab" with "maxi pad". Never Bareheaded: He's always seen in a collection of nice beanie caps.

characters.name: Handi-Man
characters.description: Catchphrase: "Never underestimate the power of the handicapped!" Evil Twin: He's given one via a villain named Doctor Naughty who wreaks havoc on the city trying to ruin his reputation. Expy: OfSuperman, right down to the same origins and Clark Kent-esque occupation. Handicapped Badass: It goes without saying. He is quite capable of defending himself and others in spite of his cerebral palsy. Paper-Thin Disguise: His Clark Kent disguise has him wearing glasses and a shirt and pants with his Handi-Man costume on underneath.

characters.name: Wanda Wayne
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: One of the most infamous examples. Between her aggressive approach and less-than-stellar looks, men are completely turned off by her. Bag of Holding: Her purse holds much more than one could imagine anditems for every occasion,from Vaseline, two types of gas for a car to even aMagical Defibrillator. Black Gal on White Guy Drama: Averted. When stalked by a White Dracula, she insists that she doesn't mind "a little milk in her coffee". Gag Lips: Jamie Foxx pushes up his already full lips to give Wanda her signature snarl. Who's Your Daddy?: A fifth season story arc finds her giving birth to a baby girl and never learning the father in spite of her insistence on finding him.

characters.name: Ed Cash and Carl Pathos
characters.description: Bestiality Is Depraved: Pathos asks Cash if "the big B" is covered in his discount sin plan. Cash responds that Pathos has been a "bad boy" in an exaggerated goat voice. Blackmail:Cash does this to Congress to get out of a congressional investigation into their finances.Another sketch has them threaten to call the spouses of the parishioners who have confessed to cheating. The donations pour in as a result. Cash does this to Congress to get out of a congressional investigation into their finances. Another sketch has them threaten to call the spouses of the parishioners who have confessed to cheating. The donations pour in as a result. Corrupt Church: For starters, they literally sell forgiveness.

characters.name: Frenchie
characters.description: The Charmer: In spite of his wild party boy antics, he'll always charm most of the people in the room in the end,especiallythe women. Comically Missing the Point: He never seems to quite comprehend that many people consider him a nuisance. Disco Dan: He still dresses like it's the mid-1980s (jheri curl, tight spandex suit, tall leather boots, etc.) The Friend Nobody Likes: Many of his supposed friends or connections that he has are embarassed by his unfiltered personality and wild persona. Leitmotif:Every Episode Endinghas him playing an instrumental of "Super Freak" byRick James.

characters.name: AlMacAfee
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: To the one teacher, Ruth. She's clearly not interested in him, but he still incessantly flirts with her. Boisterous Weakling: His attempts at initiating a fight always ends with him on the losing end. Comically Missing the Point: He always jump to conclusions and unnecessarily harasses or bullies the school, especially hapless nerdy studentHartinson.At the same time, he's blind to other people's motives or actions, especially those meaning to do him harm, and compliments them or waves them on their way. Evil Cripple: Downplayed as he isn't necessarily "evil", but he is a rather annoying and unlikable guy who has shattered hip. Jerkass: His abrasive and pretentious nature makes him hated by the student body and teachers alike.

characters.name: Magenta Thompson
characters.description: Cigarette of Anxiety: She's always seen smoking, even to the point of having an entire coffee cup full of butts. Green-Eyed Monster: She has a tendency to be embittered and jealous of other people's work. Phrase Catcher: Other characters in both her movies and in real life always shout "Outta my way, bitch!" to her. Small Name, Big Ego: Aside from unprovoked jealousy of others, especially women, she is seen being a jerk to the crew of her early morning infotainment show. White-Dwarf Starlet: She thinks her career was much larger and more impactful than it was.

characters.name: Edna Louise
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: She has a puppy love crush on fellow student Parnell, who hates her due to being loud and annoying. Attention Whore: She desperately craves attention from others, to the point of outing a girl who won the lead in a school play as adopted just so she can play her role of a princess herself. Copycat Mockery: One of many ways she annoys Parnell, often getting him in trouble in the process as well. The Dreaded: She feels that her mother is this whenever her teacher threatens to call her and report her bad behavior, prompting her to act more polite (albeit briefly). Motor Mouth: She's always talking incessantly, to the chagrin of others, especially Parnell.

characters.name: Velma Mulholland
characters.description: Deliberately Monochrome: She is always seen in black-and-white while everyone else is in color. Film Noir: Her genre of choice, speaking to everyone she encounters as they're a part of a film mystery. The Lost Lenore: She is always looking for her lost love, Johnny, who's fate is ambiguous (he's either dead or has left her). Pet the Dog: One sketch had a man break in to steal from her. Upon hearing her story as well as her not realizing his true intentions, he backs off and even returns her stolen jewelry. The Pollyanna: No matter how desperate, despairing or otherwise grim her circumstances look, she's always determined that she'll see her beloved once again.

characters.name: Cousin Elsie
characters.description: Black Sheep: Played with: in spite of her name,she isn't really related to any of the people she annoys. She's just a nosy lech. Blind Without 'Em: Implied: she wears a thick pair of glasses and often misindenifies her relatives. Drop-In Character: To everyone's shock and disappointment. She always manages to show up to housewarming parties, graduations and funerals in spite of other people's efforts to hold the events in secret. Extreme Omnisexual:Yes.She's willing to sleep with or at least force a kiss onto women, men, teenagers, coma patients and even the dead. The Friend Nobody Likes: Obviously, given her creepiness and obnoxious personality.

characters.name: Mr. & Mrs. Brooks
characters.description: Awful Wedded Life: Natch. Even when they're not trying to murder the other one, they still relentlessly snark at each other some rather vicious insults. Catchphrase: They'll both say in unison "And we'restilltogether!" More Deadly Than the Male: Mrs. Brooks' plans to kill Mr. Brooks are more common while his aremore elaborate. Not What It Looks Like: They always end up in a compromising position that appears to be sexual in natire instead of in a killing pose whenever they're caught by an outsider. They just roll with it and pretend that they are loving. Your Mom: Both are fond of these jokes towards the other one, insulting their mothers in addition to their looks, ages and other real or perceived shortcomings.

characters.name: Grandpa JackMcGee
characters.description: Alcohol-Induced Idiocy: Certainly. He's so wasted that he routinely embarrasses himself and his family, nearly burns down his kitchen making canned pork and beans without removing it first and can't tell the difference between his own mother being dead or just in an alcoholic coma (though neither does the rest of the family). Alcoholic Parent: Possibly theTrope Codifierin addition to being anabusive one. He's a drunken and unlikable mess that his children all hate. Big, Screwed-Up Family: Yes. Between him and his mother being angry and abusive alcoholics, his daughter being a slut and everyone else hating/disowning him, theMcGeefamily qualifies. Catchphrase: "PORK AND BEANS!" and to a lesser extent "I know what itis!" Drunk Driver: Par for the course. One episode has his son and other male relatives stop him from doing so.

characters.name: The "Why?" Lady
characters.description: Breaking the Fourth Wall: She will often appear out of nowhere to freeze the scene and ask why such stereotypes are always being seen in society (Black people always singing and dancing in commercials for basic, unrelated items, reporters always talking to the most ignorant and foolish of Black people, etc.) Catchphrase: The title drop of "Why?" No Name Given: She is never officially named. Stop Being Stereotypical: Something she stresses in all of her appearances.

characters.name: Ice Poe
characters.description: Gentleman Thief: Subverted. His poetry is good and he initially has a calm and polite manner, but it's only used to steal other people's belongings. Paper Tiger: In spite of his threats of violence, he's easily frightened away by the professor who makes genuine threats against him, even giving up everything that he already procured from others. To the Pain: What he uses to get what he wants, as well as whatthe professor uses to get him to give up his goods or go away. Would Harm a Senior: Even if she were a sweet old lady. Would Hit a Girl: Yes. He would also pimp out a girl, which is much worse.

characters.name: Lashawn
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: One of her trademarks. When a new trainee at a hair salon kept colossally messing up simple tasks, she finally asked if his IQ was V05. Jerkass: Even though many of the people she encounters are ignorant or annoying, her approach is still unnecessarily abrasive and rude. New Job as the Plot Demands: She changes jobs frequently, having worked at a fast food place, a museum, a hair salon and at the makeup counter of a department store. Sassy Black Woman: Indeed. Her sass is reserved for everyone, including coworkers, her employers and even the customers. Surrounded by Idiots: She thinks so, anddepending on the environment, she could be right.
